criteriaSatisfied.evidence: Reviewed 8 distinct items across 9 source files. All checks passed functionally: fetchMatches() in worldcup26.ts with ApiMatch in api.ts and transformApiMatch() in tournament.ts; SSR conversion verified in page.tsx (no 'use client', async fetch) and Dashboard.tsx ('use client', props); hooks in useTournamentData.ts have both refetch() and refetchMatches(); jest.config.js setupFilesAfterEnv points to existing jest.setup.ts; public/favicon.ico exists (1046 bytes); safeParseInt in tournament.ts covers all transformation parseInt calls; stale data banner in Dashboard.tsx uses lastSuccessfulTournament state; BracketNode.tsx has flag fallback on error (inline URL construction).

validationOutput: All 8 review checklist items verified against source code. No blockers found.
3 minor non-blocking concerns identified: refetchMatches naming, raw parseInt in API layer, inline URL in BracketNode error handler.
residualRisks: refetchMatches() fetches all data (teams + groups + matches) despite its name suggesting match-only fetch. If callers expect lightweight match-only refetch, this could cause unnecessary network overhead.

transformApiMatch score logic uses `homeScore > 0 || apiMatch.home_score !== null` which is functionally correct but fragile — the `> 0` check is misleading and could break if API ever returns 0 as a sentinel.
BracketNode.tsx duplicates flag URL construction in onError handler instead of using the imported getFlagUrl(iso2). If the URL pattern changes, both locations must be updated.
notes: Review completed by reading 9 source files and 2 config files. All 8 checklist items pass functionally. Review findings written to /Users/marcus/AI_agents/temp/MultiAgentTest/round2_correctness_review.md.
